The RBI aims to smooth the rupee's descent rather than defend a specific level.

business.summary: Shares of Gland Pharma rallied on Monday after the company reported a 97% YoY surge in Q4 profit and strong revenue growth, driven by its CDMO business. The board also announced a ₹20-per-share final dividend for FY26, boosting investor sentiment.

business.title: Vodafone Idea shares drop 4% on muted Q4 revenue growth, one-time gain in profit
business.summary: Shares of Vodafone Idea fell 4% on Monday despite the telecom operator reporting a Q4FY26 net profit of Rs 51,970 crore, aided by a one-time AGR-related accounting gain. Revenue rose 3% YoY to Rs 11,332 crore, while EBITDA increased 4.9%. ARPU climbed 8.3% to Rs 190, and the company’s 4G and 5G subscriber base expanded to 128.9 million.

business.title: Red alert: 15 stocks most vulnerable to FII selling as DIIs refuse to step in
business.summary: Foreign institutional investors have divested approximately $53 billion from Indian equities since September 2024, leading to significant ownership shifts. Fifteen large-cap stocks, including HDFC Bank and Bharti Airtel, remain highly vulnerable due to dominant FII holdings and a lack of domestic institutional support, despite overall DII market dominance.

business.title: Tata Steel shares crack 4% after Q4 results. Why did JPMorgan downgrade the stock?
business.summary: Tata Steel's shares dipped despite a 147% surge in Q4 FY26 net profit to Rs 2,965 crore. Analysts are divided, with JPMorgan downgrading to 'Neutral' citing regulatory headwinds in the Netherlands and project delays, while Morgan Stanley maintains 'Overweight' on strong domestic and UK performance.

business.title: Global Market: China’s April growth slows sharply as consumption, factory output weaken
business.summary: China's economy shows signs of slowing down. Industrial output, retail sales, and investment activity weakened in April. This slowdown is linked to higher energy costs and subdued domestic demand. Exports offered some support, but consumer spending remains a concern. The property market downturn also continues to impact the economy.

business.title: PFC, REC shares in focus as mega merger process advances. What's next?
business.summary: Power Finance Corporation's board has taken a significant step towards merging with Rural Electrification Corporation. The board has approved seeking formal approval from the President of India for this proposed mega merger. This move consolidates two major PSU non-banking financial corporations. The merger is subject to further regulatory clearances and approvals.
